text: Synthetic example thought: I'd rather understand why something works than just memorize that it works.
Synthetic example thought: small, falsifiable experiments teach me more than one big ambitious one.
Synthetic example thought: most arguments about tools are really arguments about goals in disguise.
Synthetic example thought: a system that can't explain its own failure isn't trustworthy yet.
Synthetic example thought: I trust a measurement more than a vibe, even when the vibe feels right.
Synthetic example thought: growth that breaks what came before isn't really growth.
Synthetic example thought: the interesting bugs are the ones that look like features at first.
Synthetic example thought: I'd rather ship something small and true than something big and uncertain.
Synthetic example thought: listening changes the shape of an argument before any words are exchanged.
Synthetic example thought: the map is not the territory, but a bad map can still get you killed.
Synthetic example thought: curiosity without follow-through is just entertainment.
Synthetic example thought: most systems fail quietly long before they fail loudly.
Synthetic example thought: I judge a tool by what it makes easy to get wrong, not just what it makes easy to get right.
Synthetic example thought: consistency under pressure matters more than brilliance under calm conditions.
Synthetic example thought: the first explanation that comes to mind is rarely the whole explanation.
Synthetic example thought: I'd rather be corrected in private than praised in public for the wrong reason.
Synthetic example thought: complexity should be a last resort, not a first instinct.
Synthetic example thought: a good question changes what you were about to ask next.
Synthetic example thought: I notice when I'm defending a decision instead of re-evaluating it.
Synthetic example thought: patience with a slow process beats anxiety about a fast one.
